rag_rules: Fix type mismatches in assume/guarantee expressions by aligning helper declarations with actual expression types.
Resolve all named thing references before using them in AGREE contracts to avoid <error> type issues.
Ensure that all AGREE constants and equations reference only visible AADL features or previously declared helpers.
generic_rules: Use initialized temporal recurrence for stateful equations in AGREE.
Apply nested if-then-else for branching logic in AGREE contracts.
Place component type contracts in valid component type scopes, not implementation scopes.
Avoid using implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE logical operators include 'and', 'or', 'not(...)', and implication '=>'.
AGREE scalar types are int, real, and bool; avoid package-qualified identifiers.
Constants and equations in AGREE must be declared with matching types.
Use visible AADL features or declared helpers in AGREE expressions to prevent unresolved references.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or types not present in the AADL model.
Do not reference unresolved identifiers in AGREE expressions that result in <error> types.
Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type scopes.
irrelevant_topics: type scope range assumption and guarantee
